thread.title: FDIC Urged to Withdraw ‘Hot Money’ Proposal Before GOP Kills It
thread.title_full: FDIC Urged to Withdraw ‘Hot Money’ Proposal Before GOP Kills It

author: Evan Weinberger
title: FDIC Urged to Withdraw ‘Hot Money’ Proposal Before GOP Kills It
text:
Banks and fintechs called on the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. to withdraw its proposed tightening of rules governing “hot money,” even as the agency’s chairman conceded the plan wouldn’t be finished ahead of President-elect Donald Trump’s second term.
The FDIC’s July [proposal](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/banking-law/us-banks-hot-money-gets-tougher-treatment-in-fdic-proposal), issued following a series of bank failures last year, would make it harder for troubled banks to strengthen their deposit bases with accounts from financial technology companies and other outside sources.
Banks and fintechs have [blasted](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/banking-law/big-banks-slam-fdics-hot-money-proposal-demand-more-data) the proposal since its release, arguing the FDIC failed to show a need to update rules that were last changed in ...
